metadata.subject: Arts Education
metadata.description: Choral Music 11 develops students as skilled ensemble singers and thoughtful musical artists. Students refine vocal technique, music literacy, and ensemble skills while exploring a broad choral repertoire that spans historical periods, world music traditions, and Indigenous song. The course deepens musicianship, fosters collaborative artistry, and connects students to choral performance as a meaningful form of cultural expression and community. Students engage in regular rehearsal, performance, and critical reflection on their artistic development.
metadata.general_description: A senior choral course developing ensemble vocal technique, music literacy, diverse repertoire study, and performance skills — building lifelong musical participation and cultural appreciation.

big_ideas.statement: Exploring and creating through the arts develops our capacity to communicate and express our understanding of the world.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students develop their capacity to use the choral medium as a means of personal and collective expression — communicating emotion, story, and cultural meaning through the disciplined craft of ensemble singing.

big_ideas.statement: Skilled artists intentionally select and combine elements of their art form to express their vision.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students make intentional artistic choices about tone, blend, phrasing, dynamics, and expression to realize a cohesive musical interpretation as an ensemble.

big_ideas.statement: Artists work within and across communities to create, share, and respond to art.
big_ideas.elaboration: Choral music is inherently communal — students explore how the ensemble relationship, community performance, and engagement with diverse musical traditions build cultural connection.

big_ideas.statement: Engaging with the arts expands our understanding of identity and culture.
big_ideas.elaboration: Through repertoire spanning diverse traditions — including First Peoples song, world choral music, and Western art music — students deepen their understanding of how music reflects cultural values and histories.

competencies.raw: Demonstrate and refine vocal technique including posture, breath support, tone production, intonation, diction, and range; perform with attention to blend, balance, phrasing, dynamics, and musical expression; demonstrate music literacy through sight-reading and score reading.
i_can_statements: I can demonstrate refined vocal technique including proper posture, breath support, resonance, and consistent intonation.
I can contribute to ensemble blend, balance, and unified phrasing as an ensemble member.
I can perform with musical expression — conveying the emotional and narrative content of choral works.
I can read and apply musical notation, including rhythm, pitch, dynamics, and expressive markings.
I can sight-read simple to moderately complex choral music.

competencies.raw: Apply critical and reflective thinking to evaluate musical performances; use musical vocabulary to describe and assess choral work; connect choral repertoire to its historical, cultural, and social contexts.
i_can_statements: I can evaluate choral performances — my own and others' — using accurate musical vocabulary.
I can connect choral repertoire to its historical period, cultural origin, and social context.
I can reflect meaningfully on my personal growth as a singer and ensemble member.
I can respond to First Peoples music with cultural respect and informed appreciation.

areas.name: Vocal Technique
areas.raw: Developing and refining vocal production across the choral range.
topics: Posture and alignment: standing and seated singing posture for optimal breath and resonance
Breathing and breath support: diaphragmatic breathing, breath management, and phrase phrasing
Tone production: resonance, vowel shaping, and register transitions (chest/head voice)
Intonation: developing accurate pitch matching, tuning within chords, and ensemble intonation
Diction: consonant and vowel clarity in English and other languages
Vocal health: hydration, warm-up practices, and care of the developing voice

areas.name: Ensemble Skills
areas.raw: Developing rehearsal and performance skills as a choral ensemble.
topics: Blend and balance: listening skills, section blending, and voice part balance
Ensemble phrasing: unified breath, phrase shaping, and musical line across sections
Dynamics: applying a full range of dynamic levels with precision and expressivity
Tempo and rhythm: ensemble precision, following the conductor, and internal pulse
Rehearsal skills: score marking, sectional rehearsal, and efficient practice strategies
Performance preparation: memorization, stage presence, and managing performance anxiety

areas.name: Music Literacy
areas.raw: Reading, interpreting, and analyzing choral scores.
topics: Notation review: staff, clefs, key signatures, time signatures, and note values
Sight-reading: solfège, numbers, and interval-based strategies for reading new music
Score analysis: identifying structural elements, form, and expressive markings
Choral texture: SATB, SSA, TTBB, and other voicing arrangements
Conducting patterns: recognizing and responding to basic and complex conducting gestures
Listening analysis: critically analyzing recordings of choral masterworks

areas.name: Repertoire and Cultural Study
areas.raw: Exploring diverse choral repertoire and its cultural contexts.
topics: Western choral tradition: Renaissance polyphony, Baroque, Classical, Romantic, and 20th century works
Contemporary choral music: modern Canadian and international choral compositions
First Peoples song: Indigenous vocal traditions, protocols, and contemporary First Nations choral works
World choral music: African, Latin American, Asian, and other global choral traditions
Sacred and secular choral music: navigating religious repertoire in public school contexts
Canadian choral composers: exploring works by Canadian choral artists

areas.name: Performance
areas.raw: Preparing and presenting formal and informal choral performances.
topics: Concert preparation: full concert programming, dress rehearsal, and performance etiquette
Solo and small ensemble performance: auditioned solos, duets, and chamber vocal work
Community performance: singing for school events, community concerts, and service
Festival participation: adjudicated festival performance and receiving professional feedback
Recorded performance: understanding recording processes and documenting choral work
Reflective performance journal: documenting growth across the semester/year
